The Cedars at Broadwell

CCN: 285221 · Grand Island, NE 68803 · Hall County

Overview

Address
800 Stoeger Drive, Grand Island, NE 68803
Phone
3083825440
Certified beds
76
Avg daily residents
61 (80% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1998-05-21
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.06 3.52 3.36 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.40 0.62 0.44 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.72 0.78 0.79 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.94 2.12 2.13 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.12 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.70
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.36
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.96
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
74.7%
annual
RN turnover
66.7%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.25
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.90
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-21 18 18 4 160 1 160
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-14 9 7 5 135 1 135
Total weighted health score
153.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (32)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

31 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. F 2025-05-21 2025-06-20
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-10-08 2024-11-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2024-05-14 2024-06-28
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2024-02-01 2024-02-16
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-05

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
